programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A young art student from Texas is found murdered in her bed with no sign of forced entry, indicating that she knew her killer.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a scientist is murdered in his home, detectives try to figure out why his wife and son were left unharmed.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When the mysterious death of a 23-year-old mother in New Mexico goes unsolved, her family searches for answers.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A college student goes missing in broad daylight from her off-campus job at a softball field; investigators receive a mysterious phone call that changes everything.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: Investigators try to determine what caused a house fire that killed the 41-year-old wife of a paramedic.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A pilot flees following a plane crash, becomes a fugitive and lives a double life for decades.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: Brittany Stork's decades-long quest to find out more about her mother's mysterious death leads to a dramatic courtroom decision; the man at the center of the case tells his side of the story.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A man claims his young wife killed herself after she is found dead in her burning home.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a Kansas prisoner escapes with the help of a prison volunteer and mother of two, investigators scramble to track the pair down.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a millionaire is found dead in the trunk of her car, authorities become suspicious of her ex-husband.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: Investigating the mysterious murder of a family patriarch.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: The investigation into a deadly home invasion involving a local business owner takes a turn when an unlikely witness comes forward; new details emerge from exclusive interviews and never-before-seen police interviews.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: After the cause of the death of Stacy Feldman in her Denver home is ruled as undetermined, friends and family join forces to piece together what happened; insiders close to the case speak out about the seven-year battle for justice.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: After an apparent home invasion in Minnesota leaves Heidi Firkus dead and her husband, Nick, hospitalized, detectives learn the young couple was facing financial troubles.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A nurse is gunned down in her home and detectives believe they have a clear suspect; as they delve deeper into the investigation new information leads them down a completely different path.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: The investigation into the death of a Florida firefighter reveals a tale of secrets, lies and infidelity.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a San Diego woman who is near death arrives at an emergency room, the FBI and local law enforcement are called in to try to solve a medical mystery and a horrific crime.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: Ohio investigators search for answers in the decades-old murder of 10-year-old Amy Mihaljevic; a retracing of Amy's steps from the day of the kidnapping.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A mother is found dead in a house fire but an autopsy reveals a brutal murder; the case goes cold for years until a shocking confession is made.

programme.desc: When a woman is abducted during her evening shift at a gift shop, her family keeps the case in the public eye for decades, until investigators finally solve the mystery.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a dog trainer with celebrity clientele disappears, investigators track down a suspect with the help of two witnesses.

programme.desc: A 21-year-old college student is found murdered; the case remains cold for nearly 40 years until a new detective uses an innovative method to catch her killer.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When remains are found amid the rubble after a house fire, authorities soon discover the incident was no accident.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A mother searches for answers in the unsolved murder of her 14-year-old daughter until new DNA technology changes everything.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: Lester Holt reports on the mysterious 2005 disappearance of Ray Gricar, a district attorney in Pennsylvania who investigated sex-abuse claims made against Jerry Sandusky in 1998.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When hikers find a 21-year-old UCLA student's body in a Los Angeles-area train tunnel, detectives wonder if Ron's interest in an alternative religion is connected to his murder.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A Connecticut bank executive is targeted by criminals who force him to rob his own bank.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a young woman is found nearly murdered in her apartment, officials wonder if it is connected to the disappearance of a young woman in nearby Denver weeks earlier.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A soon-to-be bride is caught in the middle of a twisted murder plot that has detectives questioning if she played a role in it.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A man is shot and killed while picking up his daughter for a court-ordered visit.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a soldier vanishes from Fort Hood, her family do everything they can to bring attention to her disappearance; Secretary of the Army Ryan McCarthy speaks about the investigation and allegations of sexual assault in the military.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A foreign exchange student and soccer player's participation in a dangerous game leads to deadly consequences.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a California housewife is murdered in broad daylight, the suspected killer claims it was a burglary gone awry.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a woman disappeared without a trace, there were two possible explanations: kidnapping or murder. Careful investigation, a forensic artist, and DNA profiling enabled police to find a single suspect, who was known to prey upon vulnerable women.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1986, Edward Post left the Omni Hotel in St. Louis and went for a 40-minute jog. Before leaving, he said that he drew a bath for his wife Julie Thigpen-Post. Ed returned and called the front desk, stating that his wife had fallen in the bathtub and was not breathing and she was dead when emergency response arrived.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The police investigate when a severed leg is discovered in the garbage. Conventional means of identifying the victim are impossible, leading investigators to rely on DNA, forensic anthropology and toxicology to identify the victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 3-year-old son of parents in Dripping Springs, Texas develops a respiratory condition. Then the father develops signs of Alzheimer's disease, and an investigator reveals that their ill health is due to a form of black mold in their modern home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murder of 9-year-old Jessica Knott is investigated. Investigators use a garbage bag to connect suspect James Edward Crow, Jr. to the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, a high school girl vanishes after attending a party. The prime suspect had not one but two alibi witnesses for the night she disappeared. A solid alibi can often overcome much circumstantial evidence. But forensic evidence is another matter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, Robert Curley was diagnosed with Guillain_Barr_ syndrome when he arrived at the hospital with excruciating leg pains. However, doctors became suspicious during Robert's second admission and tested him for Thallium poisoning, which proved positive and Robert died shortly thereafter. Autopsy showed that Robert ingested more poison a few days before his death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1987, a human skull and some bones were discovered at a Boy Scout Camp near Farmington, Missouri. A facial reconstruction aided the investigators in this case. Once the victim was identified as Bun Chee Nyhuis, her husband Richard H. Nyhuis, was no longer just a person of interest.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation into the kidnapping and rape of an 18-year-old Ohio woman is aided when the victim returns hours after the incident occurred. With her help, the police are able to track the assailant Craig Bailey to his front door.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On the night of May 22, 1992, Betty Wilson returned home after a meeting. She walked up the stairs to the bedroom and discovered her husband, lying in a pool of blood. Jack Wilson had obviously been murdered... but how? And by whom? Even the experts couldn't agree.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police in the Great Plains are called about a dead body, they do a background check on the victim which leads them into a bizarre web of homeless drifters, cattle auctions and bad checks _ all fronted by elderly couple Ray and Faye Copeland.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Details the disappearance of Melissa Brannen, a five-year-old girl who went missing from a Christmas party in 1989. The fibers on her clothes were the only means for police to determine her abductor. Police grew suspicious of Cal Hughes who was washing his clothes at 1am when they came to his home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Computers aid the police and FBI in solving a string of crimes in St. Louis.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappears without a trace. Even though foul play is suspected, the leads don't pan out and the trail turns cold. Twenty years would pass before police are able to link a clump of hair discovered in an isolated area with the missing woman.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Rosemary Anderson is found dead on the side of the road, her boyfriend becomes the prime suspect. He eventually confessed to her murder, but so did another man _ serial killer, Eric Edgar Cooke.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murders of an elderly couple leave police stumped until, two years later, they receive a major break. A jeweler looking through old newspaper clippings recognizes a necklace the female victim wore as having been pawned in her own shop.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 2-year-old boy's brain seizures and breathing problems baffle doctors until they learn the mother exhibits signs of Munchausen syndrome by proxy, a rare condition that led her to physically abuse her son and contributed to her daughter's death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The disappearance of Philadelphia college athlete Aimee Willard is investigated after her car was found on the roadside. When her body is found, the police find unusual marks on her body and DNA evidence that eventually lead to her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In a quiet village in Great Britain, a farmer came upon a chilling sight. Impaled on his fence post was a severed lamb's head along with a note which read, "You next." The author of the note didn't elaborate on why the farmer had been targeted, but between the lines, he'd said plenty.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1983, two young boys were found tied and murdered with similar wounds within three months of each other in two Nebraska towns. The rope used to tie the boys was unique. Another clue turned up when one of the boy's brother was hypnotized and remembered a uniformed man in a tan car.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappeared and her husband became the prime suspect -- especially after police found a huge bloodstain on the carpet of their bedroom. When they sprayed the bedroom with Luminol, they discovered it was awash with blood spatter. Complex DNA testing proved it was the wife's blood. Now all they had to do was find her body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1990, Shirley Andronowich's body was found one morning after being brutally murdered and mutilated in Winnipeg, Manitoba. Witnesses told police Shirley and her husband Ed were seen fighting in a bar the night before. Police first suspected Ed and even arrested him after he confessed to the murder. However, all forensic evidence proved that Ed had actually nothing to do with the murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of Katie Poirier is stymied by the lack of a body, but they find a few charred remains including a tooth. The tooth's filling contains unique properties that help investigators identify her remains. Further forensic evidence is then used to convict Donald Blom of her murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: 1984 Rajneeshee bioterror attack: investigators uncover a plot to overthrow the government after a unique strain of salmonella makes people ill.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Heavy rains and snow run-off carry manure into Milwaukee's water supply in 1993, leading to an epidemic of gastrointestinal illness affecting 400,000 people.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A neighbor watched as a man stabbed his wife, pushed her into the swimming pool and held her head under water. The husband, however, had no recollection of the crime. A jury would have to decide between evidence and the mysteries of the mind.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation into a brutal attack on two boys near a pond relies on evidence fished out of the murky water. It was the first case where Diatom evidence was used to place a suspect at the scene of the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Hikers near Anchorage, Alaska discovered a body wrapped in sheets which were edged in orange stitching. Clinging to the sheet, they also discovered a tuft of red carpet fibers _ threads of evidence which led them straight to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a 6-year-old disappears, her father becomes the prime suspect; he fails a polygraph test and even confesses, but 10 years later, the investigation gets turned on its head.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1988, when a patient dies unexpectedly in the office of California neurologist Richard Boggs, police begin what they think will be a routine investigation. Soon they uncover a bizarre story of corpse stealing, fake identity and sexual perversion.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman's death in Pennsylvania triggers a homicide investigation into another woman's death in North Carolina. The similarities in the cases were striking, and examiners must determine if the suspect's story about accidental drowning is all wet.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, the Amtrak Railroad experienced the deadliest train crash in United States history when the Sunset Limited derailed while crossing Alabama's Big Bayou Canot bridge. Forty-seven passengers and crew were killed; scores more were injured.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1992, 22-year-old Dawn Bruce is brutally murdered in her Virginia apartment. Investigators notice a blood smear on a pillow case that appears to have been made by one of the killer's fingers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Krista Harrison is the 11-year-old murder victim of Robert Anthony Buell. There are few clues to the killer's identity, but authorities are intrigued by unusual orange fibers embedded in clothing found near the victim. Months go by and the case goes cold, until a van turns up with what appear to be the same orange fibers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, Police received a call from Darlie Routier telling them of her two sons' murders. However, police were unsure about the validity of Darlie's story and began an intensive search of the crime scene. The police determined who the killer was by analyzing blood spatter, doing behavioral profiling and analyzing Darlie's 911 call.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On September 17, 1984, in a suburb of Tucson, Arizona, eight-year-old Vicki Hoskins left home on her pink bicycle to mail a letter for her mother. She never returned, but her slightly damaged bicycle was found nearby. Investigators turned to forensic science, in the hope it would tell them not only what happened to Vicki, but also who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Deadly Knowledge The investigation into a missing college coed leads police to discover that, unbeknownst to her friends, she lived a bizarre double life, attending school by day and working as a call-girl by night. When her body is found, investigators gather evidence that implicates her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For years, a woman suffered from what appeared to be the unpleasant side effects of lithium, a drug prescribed to treat bipolar disorder. When she died, investigators had to determine if her death was due to natural causes, suicide, or murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The story of firefighter Michelle Baker is related. After meeting Dr. Maynard Muntzing, she became pregnant by him. After this news, she was ill whenever they were together. The coincidences led her to suspect that he was doing her harm.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Some of the refugees who sought asylum in the United States after World War II lied about the atrocities in which they'd participated. Years later, when a high-ranking religious figure was suspected of war crimes, there seemed to be no way to prove his guilt or innocence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Investigators sift through the ashes of a fire which killed a 40-year-old woman. The fire appeared to have been started by an unattended cigarette. On closer look, forensic scientists discover the cause was far more complicated.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A surveillance camera captures a murder on tape, but the image of the killer is too degraded to make a positive I.D. Police employ forensic science and modern technology to nab their man.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Early one morning in a deserted area outside of Phoenix, a motorcyclist discovered the body of a young woman. She had been beaten, bound, strangled and possibly raped. The nearby plants would tell investigators more about the killer than any other single piece of evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 19-year-old woman was found dead in her car; the scene had been staged to make murder appear to be suicide. During their investigation, police discovered the victim was involved in a love quadrangle, giving several people a motive to kill her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Witnesses, investigators, and medical experts discuss how forensic evidence was used to solve notable true crime cases in this long-running series.

programme.title: Atlanta Homicide
programme.desc: 21-year-old new mother, Sparkle Rai, is found by her boyfriend strangled in their apartment. He instantly becomes the only suspect, but he swears he didn’t do it. Quinn and Velazquez investigate and uncover a surprising new suspect from Sparkle’s past.

programme.title: Payback
programme.desc: When young expectant parents are found gunned down outside an apartment complex, Memphis police are shocked when the father-to-be�s family members theorize the couple�s deaths are the result of a dispute over bundles of hair.

programme.title: Sins of the City
programme.desc: After a beloved family of seven is firebombed in their sleep in the Baltimore neighborhood of Oliver, the Baltimore City Police fall under intense scrutiny, ultimately bearing as much blame from the community as the killer himself.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: Shortly after a large worker’s comp settlement gave one family a chance at a good life, their husband and father is found dead in his truck. As police expose dark secrets, they’re led to a cold-blooded killer nobody expected.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: When the body of a young woman is found in a vacant lot, friends she was out with the night she disappeared tell police they saw her leave in a mysterious black car. Was she a victim of a predator – or are her friends concealing a terrible secret?

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: A Houston woman with a heart of gold is found shot to death, and police consider a love triangle may have taken a fatal turn. Detectives delve into her love life, they uncover a possessive suspect who decided if he couldn't have her, no one would.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: A young dancer believes she’s found her soulmate when she meets a handsome man who promises to make her a star. Desperate to earn his love, she makes a deal with the devil – and for a shot at the spotlight it will cost her body and her soul.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: A single mom rekindles a relationship with her high school sweetheart after fate brings them together. But their “happily ever after” is doomed when a risky idea to save their family ends in bloodshed…and their loyalty to each other is tested.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: Sparks fly when a young mother reunites with her childhood crush. But her new man is guarding a deadly secret that will put her, and her son�s, lives in danger. When she gets caught in the crosshairs, she must choose�either save her man or herself.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: After back-to-back tragedies have left her estranged from her family, a young woman finds the love and acceptance she�d always wished for, but when her man wants more for them, she�ll be roped into a devilish plot to take back what she left behind.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: When a young woman and the love of her life begin preparing a future together, they�re struck by tragedy with a miscarriage. And as the cracks between them begin to form, one woman will have to decide how far she�ll go to create a perfect family.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe

programme.title: The David Rubenstein Show: Peer to Peer Conversations
programme.desc: Financier and philanthropist David Rubenstein talks with his peers -- leaders of some of America's biggest companies -- in this interview show that aims to answer the question of what makes a great leader. Rubenstein uses his chats to explore successful leadership skills through the personal and professional choices made by the business world's most influential people. Rubenstein travels across the country to find out about the business leaders' stories and uncover information about their paths to success. The interview subjects include Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates, Berkshire Hathaway chairman Warren Buffett, and Pepsi CEO Indra Nooyi.

programme.desc: Bloomberg The Asia Trade - bringing global investors everything you need to know as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers.

programme.title: Bloomberg: The China Show
programme.desc: From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and David Ingles give global investors insight into the world's second-biggest economy, delivering in-depth discussions with newsmakers.

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: Asia
programme.desc: In an effort to provide extensive coverage of worldwide financial markets, Bloomberg features several regional shows under the "Bloomberg Markets" banner, with each focusing on that geographic area's markets. This Asian-focused version is hosted by Shery Ahn, David Ingles, Haidi Lun, Rishaad Salamat and Angie Lau. They report the latest business news and speak with the likes of political and business leaders to help investors make investment decisions that build their portfolios.

programme.title: Bloomberg Daybreak: Europe

programme.title: Bloomberg The Opening Trade
programme.desc: The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Kriti Gupta.

programme.title: The Pulse with Francine Lacqua

programme.title: Bloomberg Brief

programme.title: Bloomberg Surveillance

programme.title: Bloomberg Open Interest

programme.title: Bloomberg Technology
programme.desc: Technology is not only becoming an increasingly important part of people's lives but also of the American economy. "Bloomberg Technology" takes a look at how the latest happenings in the tech industry are affecting the U.S. markets. Analysis, commentary, interviews and interactive graphics are among the staples of the show that help viewers understand the impact the technology sector has on the economy. The show, which was originally titled "Bloomberg West," is anchored by Emily Chang in San Francisco.

programme.title: Bloomberg ETF IQ
programme.desc: Bloomberg's Matt Miller, Katie Greifeld and Eric Balchunas are joined by leaders in the market, providing critical intelligence to finance advisers and investors of ETFs.

programme.title: LEADERS with Francine Lacqua:Alexander Stubb, President of Finland
programme.desc: What drives the world’s most influential minds? In Leaders with Francine Lacqua, Bloomberg’s award-winning anchor sits down with the decision-makers shaping our future to explore the personal stories behind their public personas. Insightful, cinematic and elegantly crafted, Leaders goes beyond the headlines to reveal the human side of leadership.

programme.title: Misión extrajudicial
programme.desc: Sebastián Ornelas, además de ser el amo y señor de todo el poblado, es el hombre más buscado por las autoridades. Un día, su guapa mujer conoce a un judicial, quién está a cargo de su captura, y ahí comienza la verdadera cacería.

programme.title: Chicas bien
programme.desc: En un intento de pasar un buen rato y olvidar el posible divorcio de sus padres, Fernanda decide invitar a sus mejores amigas a pasar un fin de semana en su casa de campo.

programme.title: Monstruosamente solo
programme.desc: A un hombre le rompen el corazón y decide encerrarse en su departamento para estar solo, pero no puede evitar el apoyo de quienes lo quieren e incluso del monstruo imaginario de su infancia.

programme.title: Retrato familiar
programme.desc: Dos secuestradores irrumpen en la casa de una familia de la alta sociedad mexicana. Los criminales necesitan dinero para destinarlo a la consecución de una información que alguien les ha exigido.

programme.title: East L.A. King (Español)
programme.desc: El líder de una pandilla en el este de Los Ángeles va a la cárcel después de un trato fallido. La prisión lo cambia, pero su hijo cae en pandillas.

programme.title: La Suburban de las Monjas
programme.desc: Cuatro hermosas monjas se dedican a conquistar a los hombres con sus sonrisas celestiales y siembran el terror con sus armas de alto poder.

programme.desc: Mariana y Julián lo tienen todo: dos hijos, éxito y amor mutuo. Entonces intentan un matrimonio abierto, y la situación se pone fea.

programme.title: El triunfo del mojado
programme.desc: Pablo, un campesino, viaja a Estados Unidos en busca de una vida mejor. Pero el plan se complica cuando descubre que su familia ha sido secuestrada.

programme.title: Moisés y los diez mandamientos
programme.desc: La historia de Moisés desde su nacimiento y llegada a manos de la princesa Henutmire hasta llevar a su pueblo a la Tierra Prometida. Su crianza como un verdadero príncipe egipcio junto a su tío Ramsés.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas
programme.desc: Song-Yi encuentra a Min-Joon sangrando por una lesión en la cabeza. El abogado Jang llega para advertirle que no se encariñe con Min-Joon.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas
programme.desc: Song-yi finalmente descubre el secreto de Min-joo. Además, él revela que se preguntó si Song-yi es la reencarnación de Yi-hwa, una chica que conoció 400 años antes.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas
programme.desc: Song-yi está en la sala de emergencias después de un accidente que Jae-kyung planeó. Min-joon piensa culparse por todas las acciones malvadas de Jae-kyung.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas
programme.desc: Min-joon confronta a Jae-kyung sobre todas las vidas que ha destruido. Song-yi le dice a Min-joon que se aleje de ella, pero hace lo opuesto.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas
programme.desc: Min-joon se desploma después de besar a Song-yi. Hwi-kyung se despierta y pretende que no sabe nada de los planes de Jae-kyung. Min-joon le dice a Song-yi que debe irse.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Reyhan y Mahinur van a la granja para hablar con Fatma. Metin defiende a Öykü y golpea a Siman. Orhan se enoja al saber que Meyati pagó dos millones más por la granja de Fatma.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Orhan le avisa a Cüneyt que acepta formar parte de la cooperativa. Selim acude a la inauguración de la fundación y sorprende a Busra. Orhan se entera de que el cantante no llegará a tiempo así que Kadir toma su lugar.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Sinan busca a Öyku y al ver que está con Metin, se presenta como su novio. Orhan y Busra se besan y Selim los ve. Öyku va a casa de Metin para disculparse, pero Mahinur es dura con ella y le pide alejarse.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Alguien arroja una piedra con un mensaje amenazante a la ventana de la casa de Yesim. Mahinur amenaza a Öykü. Selim intenta decirle la verdad a la madre de Busra. Besime alucinando con Sermin.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Metin encuentra una nota de Öykü en el invernadero. Los niños del colegio piden autógrafos a Kadir después de la fiesta. Öykü está triste porque se imagina que Metin no la ha perdonado.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Para protegerlo, Jade toma aire y fuerzas para decirle a Lucas que no lo ama y lo echa de la casa. Ella se siente destruida y, con más fastidio hacia Said, aplica el plan de aburrirlo.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Jade no se rinde y le quema la ropa a Said, pero él ya se dio cuenta de sus intenciones de desesperarlo y le grita que jamás le va a dar el divorcio. Ella le suplica, pero no consigue nada.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Con dolor y resignación, Lucas acepta su destino con Marisa y el bebé que van a tener. Jade se entera de que Lucas va a ser padre y rompe en llanto. Cristina averigua por el papá del niño de Dora.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Jade quiere otra esposa para Said y habla con Muna, una mujer bella y soltera, le dice que Said busca otra mujer. Cristina invita a Leonardo a la fiesta de Daniel, Albieri está nervioso.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: En cuanto ve a su hija, Lucas se conmueve y olvida todas sus discusiones con Marisa. Albieri inventa otra mentira, dice que el embrión fue implantado en otro vientre, no en el de Dora.

programme.title: El clon

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas

programme.title: The Spark
programme.desc: Dancer. Educator. Diplomat. Peacemaker. Aysha Upchurch is a force of nature. But it’s her gift for inspiring students that makes her the real star.

programme.title: Ed & Day in The 'Burgh
programme.desc: Ed and Day explore the many sides of Lawrenceville. From sword fighting and thrifting to making wine and burgers, the guys experience the beauty of this eclectic neighborhood.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: In historic Ybor City, different cultures bring out the best in one another. Enjoy an authentic Cuban sandwich or dine at a restaurant now on its fifth generation of ownership. After chicken yoga (yes, that’s a thing!), treat yourself to a hand-rolled cigar.

programme.title: Finding Adventure

programme.title: Only In

programme.desc: Scott's life revolves around everyone's favorite four-legged friends, from rescuing and caring for countless dogs, his spirit is unwavering. Today Meaghan gives back a day full of moments for him and his beloved canine companion.

programme.title: Caught On Clips
programme.desc: The wildest, wackiest, and most jaw-dropping videos. From dangerous severe weather, to heart-stopping close calls and adorable animal antics - all caught on camera.

programme.title: Ecosystems
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau visits the Everglades of Florida and travels these waters by airboat to discover wildlife. Then we visit Yellowstone and explore this unique ecosystem that remains relatively undisturbed. We'll also look at planet Earth as one giant ecosystem, and examine the inter-dependency of all living things.

programme.title: Extreme Weather
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau ventures to Mt. Washington in New Hampshire, a place known as the "Home of the World's Worst Weather." We visit the "Wall of Wind" in Florida where scientists simulate hurricane force winds. In Colorado, we follow a scientific research team pursuing a tornado. We travel to Texas Tech, where they explore the destruction of debris thrown by tornadoes.

programme.title: Animal Athletes
programme.desc: In this episode, we'll examine some of the animal kingdom's fastest sprinters, like the Cheetah. Also, the mysteries of animal intelligence are revealed as we take a look at Dolphins and Orangutans.

programme.title: The Predators
programme.desc: In this episode, we examine some of the world's most fearsome predators. Find out why Owls are the only birds that can fly without making any sound. And how the Leopard uses its amazing night vision to catch its prey.

programme.title: Science On Fire
programme.desc: Join host Nick Uhas and his guests as they kindle our curiosity about the science behind fire. This week's experiments investigate how you can burn metal using only a 9 volt battery, what fire can teach us about calories, and why a little bit of sodium can cause a gigantic explosion.

programme.title: Like Button
programme.desc: When first presented with the like button (then dubbed "the awesome button") Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg had a simple response: "No." He worried that a quick thumbs up or down would discourage more meaningful online interaction. Eventually, his team changed his mind and transformed the world.

programme.title: WALKIES: Best Walks
programme.desc: This week on Photowalks, Jefferson Graham lists his favorite walking tours. Follow along for the best places to ditch the car and walk around your vacation destination, taking the best photos with your phone.

programme.title: America 250 Road Trip Itineraries
programme.desc: Photowalks host Jefferson Graham takes a road trip through American history. He's on a journey to take the best photos following America's founding in 1776, starting in Boston and ending in Washington, DC.

programme.title: Collateral Damage
programme.desc: Highlights include: "What were you Thinking?", "Watch Your Step!", "Your Own Worst Enemy" and "The Top 10 Times You Didn't Realize There Was a Glass Door There".

programme.title: Recreational Activities
programme.desc: Vacationers hitting the ski slopes, gymnasts trying to stick the landing, funny cruise ship fails and more are featured as people enjoy their leisure activities.

programme.title: Life Goes On
programme.desc: People who should've quit while they were ahead are featured, including a boy getting rolled over by a huge snowball, kids swinging like Tarzan and a man dressing up like a mermaid.
